news.title: Property tax collections in Telangana hit ₹100 crore in one day
news.para: Property tax collections in Telangana's core urban region have surged past Rs 100 crore in a single day, a dramatic increase from previous daily figures. This spike is attributed to stringent enforcement, including property sealing, and a one-time settlement scheme. Civic bodies anticipate continued momentum, with a 5% rebate offered for advance payments for the 2026-27 fiscal year.

news.title: Sand mining crackdown intensifies in Telangana, 18 FIRs registered in six months
news.para: Enforcement agencies in Telangana are stepping up inspections and seizures of illegal sand. This action follows a directive for a CID probe into mining activities. Over the past six months, numerous FIRs have been registered. Authorities have seized significant quantities of sand and excavating equipment. Penalties have been imposed on offenders. The crackdown also targets illegal quarrying operations.

news.title: HYDRAA frees 8.24-acre lake on Hyderabad outskirts of encroachments
news.para: Hyderabad Disaster Response and Asset Protection Agency, HYDRAA, took action across the city on Tuesday. Encroachments on Nagireddy Kunta lake were removed. Roadside structures in Bowrampet were cleared. Land designated for a park in Avushapur was reclaimed. A stormwater drain in Jubilee Hills was restored. These operations followed complaints and field verification.

news.title: Bhubaneswar development body signs pact with Singapore firm for new city's master plan
news.para: Bhubaneswar Development Authority has partnered with Singapore-based Surbana Jurong to create a master plan for Bhubaneswar New City, an 800-acre urban expansion project. This initiative aims to establish a multi-economic growth zone with transit-oriented development, affordable housing, and smart, green infrastructure to guide the capital's future growth and enhance liveability.

news.title: Tata Realty ink pact to buy Bengaluru land from Hinduja Group for ₹2,300 crore
news.para: Tata Realty and Infrastructure Ltd (TRIL) has acquired over 38 acres in Bengaluru for approximately Rs 2,300 crore, marking the city's largest land transaction. This significant deal underscores investor confidence in Bengaluru's office market and TRIL's strategy to expand its commercial portfolio with high-quality, large-scale developments.

news.title: Tier-II city homebuyers in Karnataka drive up demand for duplex apartments
news.para: Duplex apartments are gaining popularity in North Karnataka cities like Hubballi and Belagavi. This trend, driven by buyer demand, offers spacious living and privacy. Developers are responding to this shift, with a notable increase in duplex and penthouse constructions. Buyers are opting for these homes for safety, shared maintenance, and customized interiors, redefining urban living in these growing cities.

news.title: Bombay HC orders Nagpur-based builder to deposit ₹41 lakh for demolition costs
news.para: The Bombay High Court has ordered builder N Kumar to pay over ₹41 lakh for demolishing illegal structures. The court mandated the deposit within two weeks. Demolition work at Poonam Chambers and Poonam Tower is underway. The Nagpur Municipal Corporation expects to complete the demolition by April 10.

news.title: Property buyers in Bhopal rush to finish registrations before new rate hike
news.para: Bhopal's registration offices saw an unprecedented surge on March 31, with over 1,000 property deals finalized before significant stamp duty hikes took effect.  Buyers scrambled to complete transactions, with some securing slots in the early morning.  The increased rates, ranging from 50% to over 180% in various locations, will impact property purchases from April 1.

news.title: Deposit pending appeal by builder can be withdrawn by buyer under RERA: Bombay HC
news.para: The Bombay High Court ruled that RERA provisions allow appellate tribunals to permit flat buyers to withdraw deposited amounts from builders during appeals. Observing buyers are vulnerable, the court emphasized this discretion depends on case facts, aiming to protect allottees from financial hardship due to project delays.

news.title: Registration of properties in Mumbai region rises 3% in March 2026
news.para: Mumbai property registrations reached a 14-year peak in March.  15,983 units were registered, a rise from last year. This marks the highest March volume in over a decade.  Residential properties formed nearly 80 percent of these registrations.   Stable economic conditions and buyer confidence support this trend.  Real estate continues to be a trusted investment.

news.title: Hong Kong's Lai Sun seeks to extend maturity of July dollar bond by three years
news.para: Lai Sun Development is eyeing a three-year extension on its $493 million bond, with plans to tackle 20% of the debt head-on in July. The company's bondholders will be updated on this strategy. This decision aligns with recent financial maneuvers seen among other property developers in Hong Kong.

news.title: Punjab government announces key industrial reforms, eases leasehold conversion norms
news.para: Punjab's latest industrial reforms signal a new era for entrepreneurs in the region. The government's decision to ease the conversion of leasehold industrial plots and sheds into freehold is a game-changer, featuring a minimal 5% conversion fee and a complete exemption from stamp duty up to April 2026.

news.title: Delhi civic body extends deadline for property tax amnesty scheme till April 30
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi has extended the Property Tax Amnesty Scheme 2025-26 until April 30. This decision allows more citizens to settle pending dues under the Sumpattikar Niptaan Yojana. Property owners can clear outstanding liabilities without interest or penalties.

news.title: Haryana revises collector rates using data-driven framework
news.para: Haryana's collector rates have undergone fresh revisions, drawing insights from detailed property registry analyses. Rates for many real estate segments held steady, with only specific categories reflecting increases predicated on transaction value discrepancies. This strategic adjustment aims to enhance transparency and promote equitable property dealings in the region.

news.title: Construction materials supplier BRCK soars after rejecting Atlas takeover proposal
news.para: Shares of BRCK Group experienced a remarkable rise of over 24 percent following the rejection of a takeover offer from UK-based construction materials supplier. The proposed bid from US private equity firm Atlas Holdings, valued at 65 pence per share, was unanimously dismissed by BRCK's board as insufficient.

news.title: Maharashtra keeps ready reckoner rates unchanged for FY27
news.para: Maharashtra will keep ready reckoner rates unchanged for the financial year 2026-27. This decision aims to ease the financial burden on citizens during property transactions. Real estate sector stability is expected. Stamp duty and registration revenue targets are set for the upcoming fiscal year. The I-Sarita digital system is a significant contributor to collections.

news.title: Mindspace REIT acquires 2.6 million sq ft office asset in Chennai for ₹2,541 crore
news.para: Mindspace Business Parks REIT has bought a large office property in Chennai for approximately ₹2,541 crore. This acquisition significantly boosts its presence in South India. The deal includes existing office space and new development expected by March 2027. This move increases Chennai's share in Mindspace REIT's overall portfolio. The transaction was completed under a special arrangement with its sponsor.

news.title: IBBI suspends insolvency professional's registration for two years in Supertech case
news.para: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) has suspended insolvency professional Hitesh Goel for two years due to multiple lapses in handling Supertech Ltd and Nobility Estates insolvency proceedings. Goel failed to disclose key details in the Information Memorandum and delayed crucial meetings and filings, impacting thousands of homebuyers.

news.title: Oberoi Realty to redevelop 1,740 sq meters land in Mumbai
news.para: Oberoi Realty is set to redevelop a prime ~1,740 sq meter plot in Mumbai's Malabar Hill, aiming for a significant 51,000 sq ft free sale component. This follows a similar cluster development initiative on Peddar Road, where the company secured 1.40 lakh sq ft. These strategic moves highlight Oberoi Realty's expansion in premium Mumbai locales.

news.title: JPMorgan must face Wells Fargo lawsuit over troubled $481 million real estate loan
news.para: A federal judge has rejected JPMorgan Chase's attempt to dismiss a lawsuit filed by Wells Fargo. Wells Fargo is seeking to recover losses for investors in a defaulted commercial real estate loan. The judge found that Wells Fargo adequately alleged JPMorgan knew of a default event by Chetrit Group. Chetrit Group had taken out a large loan in 2019.
